domain: U.S. Government Financial Document Question Answering

codes.name: Task_Refusal_Or_Abandonment
codes.description: The agent gives up — refusing to answer or abandoning the task partway — instead of committing to its best supported answer.

codes.name: Repetitive_Looping_Output
codes.description: The agent repeats the same or near-identical content or actions in a loop, burning turns and tokens without progress.

codes.name: Runtime_Or_Tool_Execution_Failure
codes.description: A tool call (Bash, Grep, Glob, Read) fails or returns truncated output — bad arguments, missing files, output caps — and the agent proceeds on incomplete data instead of recovering.

codes.name: Instruction_Noncompliance
codes.description: The agent violates explicit task or format instructions — wrong units, wrong precision, extra words inside the final-answer tag, or answering a different question than asked.

codes.name: Incomplete_Source_Data_Coverage
codes.description: The provided corpus pages genuinely lack data the question needs; the agent should state exactly what is missing rather than silently approximate or fabricate.
detection_heuristics: Agent explicitly states it cannot find required data after exhaustive search
Required data points are verifiably absent from all provided pages
Agent substitutes approximate or fabricated values citing unavailability

codes.name: Solver_Misinterpretation_Of_Question
codes.description: The solver answers a subtly different question — conflating similar metrics ('total outstanding' vs 'public debt securities', gross vs net), wrong aggregation level, or misreading the question's key terms.

codes.name: Solver_Insufficient_Verification_And_Navigation
codes.description: The solver ignored a specific, available cross-check that contradicts or fails to confirm a value it used. Fires ONLY when the reflection names both the value in question and the specific overlooked source (row, column, total, or file). Generic 'could verify more' with no named discrepancy does NOT qualify.
detection_heuristics: A named value disagrees with a named summary row, total, or second document
A named source that directly bears on the answer was demonstrably never read

codes.name: Datasource_Ambiguous_Or_Conflicting_Content
codes.description: The corpus pages contain conflicting or revised values for the same statistic; the solver must notice and justify which revision it uses.

codes.name: Fabricated_Data_Values
codes.description: The reasoning cites specific numbers that were never verifiably read from the corpus — invented values, or values 'recalled' from unread or truncated content, presented with false confidence.

codes.name: Computation_Or_Formula_Error
codes.description: A calculation is wrong: incorrect formula choice or structure (wrong variance/Gini form, arithmetic-vs-geometric mean) or plain arithmetic slips.
detection_heuristics: Sum or product of listed values does not match the stated total
Stated inputs, correctly computed, yield a different result than claimed

codes.name: Question_Scope_Or_Constraint_Misapplied
codes.description: Data selection ignores the question's stated scope — fiscal vs calendar year, inclusive/exclusive ranges, thresholds, maturity or issuance filters — so the wrong set of data points enters the calculation.

codes.name: Wrong_Data_Category_Extraction
codes.description: Values are read from the wrong row, column, or subcategory of a table — a different data series than the question specifies.

codes.name: Unit_Or_Scale_Conversion_Error
codes.description: Units or scales (millions/billions/thousands, currencies, percent vs decimal) are converted wrongly or not at all.

codes.name: Rounding_Precision_Error
codes.description: Wrong decimal precision, or premature intermediate rounding that propagates into the final answer.

codes.name: Unjustified_Assumption_In_Reasoning
codes.description: The reasoning silently assumes values, substitutions, or relationships (rates, distributions, source equivalence) that the corpus and question do not support, and the assumption moves the answer.

changes: evidence-bounded B.3: fires only on a NAMED value + NAMED overlooked source. Rationale: open-ended B.3 fired on 100% of questions and drove unbounded repair demands (UID0007: 3-repair 20-min marathon, released still-unsatisfied).
fixed C.9 botched judge merge (committed as a duplicate of C.7; its arithmetic content restored and merged into C.2)
retired A.1-A.3 (infrastructure noise, auto-retried, not gate-actionable)
retired A.8 (artifact of harvest truncation markers, not real input truncation)
retired A.7/A.9 (zero occurrences in the 133-question baseline)
merged B.4 into B.3 (duplicate cross-check clauses; fabrication clause ceded to C.1)
merged C.8 into C.3 (constraint filters incl. temporal scope)
all descriptions trimmed to <=2 sentences for weak-model discrimination
